key_insight: Cross-platform proteomics of ALS biofluids revealed many candidate proteins and showed that mass spectrometry and Olink each capture partly overlapping, partly unique biomarker signals.
clinical_relevance: These findings could help accelerate discovery of blood and CSF biomarkers and potential drug targets for amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, supporting future patient stratification and therapy development.
method: TMTpro 35-plex mass spectrometry on CSF and plasma from the Target ALS Global Natural History Study
Compared sporadic ALS samples with controls in both biofluids
Cross-validated select proteins with orthogonal immunoassays and compared results with Olink proximity extension assay data
evidence_signal: The study identified 2,875 proteins in CSF and 1,118 in plasma, with differentially expressed proteins between controls and sALS and partial overlap across platforms.
watch_item: This was an initial cross-sectional study, so biomarker candidates still need longitudinal validation and independent replication before clinical use.
paper_title: The Target ALS Global Natural History Study: Cross-platform proteomics to accelerate biofluid biomarker and drug target discovery in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis
